# elizaOS Discord - 2026-06-01

## Summary

### DeFi Integration Architecture

Smeltor (Ben) presented a multi-chain DeFi aggregation system designed for AI agents that provides a unified interface across swap, earn, perpetuals, bridge, and borrow operations. The system abstracts multiple protocols including Kamino, Aave, Morpho, Compound, and Hyperliquid behind a single integration point, returning signable calldata for non-custodial agent wallets. This approach aims to reduce boilerplate code while maintaining the non-custodial nature of agent operations across multiple chains and protocols.

### Integration Strategy Debate

A technical debate emerged regarding the optimal approach for DeFi integrations: abstraction layers versus protocol-specific implementations. N4vnt discussed building a Kamino-focused project as a go-to-market strategy to gain protocol attention and establish credibility. Smeltor questioned the community about value propositions, specifically whether the convenience of multi-protocol abstraction outweighs the granular control offered by individual protocol integrations. This reflects broader architectural decisions facing AI agent developers in the DeFi space.

### AI Agent Financial Infrastructure

Ana-Maria from NeverBell introduced infrastructure enabling AI agents to execute financial market operations through natural language interfaces. The team is seeking early testers among technical builders and agent developers to validate their approach to bridging natural language commands with financial market execution.

### Project Structure Clarification

Odilitime provided clarification on the elizaOS project structure addressing community concerns. The waifu fun platform does not have its own token, but Solace on waifu has a token called waifu. The elizaOS project remains active despite community questions about development focus and priorities.

## FAQ

**Q: Does waifu fun have its own token?**
A: No, waifu fun does not have its own token. However, Solace on waifu has a token called waifu.

**Q: Is the elizaOS project still active?**
A: Yes, the elizaOS project remains active despite community concerns about focus and development priorities.

**Q: What protocols does the multi-chain DeFi aggregation system support?**
A: The system abstracts protocols including Kamino, Aave, Morpho, Compound, and Hyperliquid behind a single integration point.

**Q: What operations does the DeFi aggregation system support?**
A: The system provides a unified interface for swap, earn, perpetuals, bridge, and borrow operations across multiple chains.

**Q: Is the DeFi aggregation system custodial?**
A: No, the system returns signable calldata for non-custodial agent wallets, maintaining the non-custodial nature of operations.

## Help Interactions

**Helper:** odilitime
**Helpee:** Community members
**Resolution:** Clarified the project structure regarding waifu fun tokens and confirmed that elizaOS remains an active project.

**Helper:** Ana-Maria (NeverBell)
**Helpee:** Technical builders and agent developers
**Resolution:** Offered early testing opportunities for AI agent financial market execution infrastructure with natural language interfaces.

## Action Items

### Technical

- Evaluate whether multi-protocol abstraction or individual protocol integrations better serve AI agent DeFi use cases (mentioned by Smeltor)
- Test NeverBell infrastructure for AI agent financial market execution through natural language interfaces (mentioned by Ana-Maria)

### Features

- Develop Kamino-focused project as go-to-market strategy to gain protocol attention (mentioned by N4vnt)

# elizaOS Discord - 2026-05-30

## Summary

### Codebase Reorganization

The plugin-solana component has been relocated and integrated into the main eliza repository. The plugin is now available as plugin-wallet under the chains/solana subdirectory, representing a significant structural change to the elizaOS codebase architecture.

### Plugin Development and Contributions

Active development is underway for a plugin-kamino integration for elizaOS. The contribution process for new plugins follows standard pull request procedures to the official repository. Previous Kamino integration work exists that includes read functionality for version 1.x, which may serve as a reference for ongoing development efforts.

### Project Status and Direction

The elizaOS project remains active despite team focus shifting to waifu.fun. Ongoing projects continue to involve the elizaOS token, and the project is not abandoned. Community concerns were raised about team commitment to the ElizaOS token following observed token sales from team-associated addresses.

## FAQ

**Q: Where is plugin-solana located now?**
A: Plugin-solana has been moved and integrated into the main eliza repository as plugin-wallet, specifically under the chains/solana subdirectory.

**Q: How can I contribute a new plugin to elizaOS?**
A: New plugins can be contributed through pull requests to the official repository.

**Q: Is elizaOS still being actively developed?**
A: Yes, elizaOS is neither dead nor abandoned. While team focus has shifted to waifu.fun, there are ongoing projects involving the elizaOS token.

**Q: Is there existing code for Kamino integration?**
A: Yes, there is code from a previous Kamino integration attempt that includes read functionality for version 1.x, which can be shared as a reference.

## Help Interactions

**Helper:** odilitime
**Helpee:** n4vnt
**Resolution:** Clarified that plugin-solana has been moved to the main eliza repository as plugin-wallet under chains/solana subdirectory. Confirmed the contribution process for the plugin-kamino development and offered to share existing Kamino integration code with read functionality for version 1.x.

**Helper:** odilitime
**Helpee:** btcordinals888
**Resolution:** Addressed concerns about project status by clarifying that elizaOS is not dead or abandoned, explaining that while team focus shifted to waifu.fun, ongoing projects continue with the elizaOS token.

## Action Items

### Technical

- Complete development of plugin-kamino for elizaOS (mentioned by n4vnt)
- Review and potentially utilize existing Kamino integration code with read functionality for version 1.x (mentioned by odilitime)

### Features

- Submit pull request for plugin-kamino contribution to official repository (mentioned by n4vnt)

ElizaOS Development Summary: June 1, 2026
---
On June 1, 2026, the ElizaOS team made significant progress across three main areas: agentic orchestration, runtime enhancements, and infrastructure stability. The most notable achievement was the launch of the /orchestrator coding workbench, which achieves parity with Claude-Code and Codex-style workflows using the opencode and Cerebras backend. Alongside this, ACP request reliability was improved by ensuring JSON-RPC error data fields are preserved during error handling, and issues with planner meta-narration and workbench UI parity were resolved. On the runtime side, a new keyless WEB_FETCH action was introduced to enable live information retrieval for non-Anthropic models, expanding the flexibility of the system. Runtime configuration was also improved by allowing the run-node entry filename to be customized, and image boot stability was increased by moving plugin-google and plugin-lifeops to optional status. Infrastructure work included resolving cloud agent sandbox routing and startup failures, streamlining deployment pipelines with environment-specific triggers and submodule guards, and fixing Windows installer naming and PGlite lock self-healing issues. A large number of pull requests remain in progress spanning core and security improvements, orchestrator and cloud enhancements, and general fixes and features. Open issues include false-positive stale build flags, coding-agent UI parity requirements, and WEB_FETCH planner routing regressions, while several issues were closed including build breaks in the develop branch, diagnostic data loss in ACP errors, and superseded planner routing for WEB_FETCH.

# Overall Project Monthly Summary (May 2026)

## Executive Summary
May 2026 was a month of foundational hardening for ElizaOS, focused on stabilizing our core framework and ensuring seamless compatibility across diverse computing environments. By resolving critical infrastructure bottlenecks and standardizing how our plugins interact with the core system, we have significantly improved the reliability and security of our AI agent deployments.

### Key Strategic Initiatives & Outcomes

**Strengthening Core Reliability and Cross-Platform Stability**
*Goal: To ensure our agents run consistently and securely on any server or operating system.*
- We hardened the agent runtime for headless environments, preventing system crashes on Linux and Windows servers ([elizaos/eliza](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza)).
- We implemented a new, secure way to manage sensitive information across different platforms, making it easier to integrate with our settings interface ([elizaos/eliza](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza)).
- We resolved complex system-level errors that previously hindered reliable agent performance in headless deployments ([elizaos-plugins/plugin-n8n-workflow](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-n8n-workflow)).

**Modernizing Our Build Architecture**
*Goal: To align our plugin ecosystem with modern web standards for better performance and easier maintenance.*
- We transitioned multiple plugins ([plugin-ollama](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-ollama), [plugin-openrouter](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-openrouter), [plugin-pdf](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-pdf), and [plugin-openai](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-openai)) to a modern, modular build structure, eliminating runtime conflicts with the core framework.

**Ensuring Secure and Reliable Integrations**
*Goal: To maintain high security and service continuity for our decentralized exchange and communication tools.*
- We migrated our Jupiter integration to the official API endpoint and implemented mandatory security key authentication to keep our decentralized trading features compliant and reliable ([elizaos-plugins/plugin-jupiter](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-jupiter)).
- We refined configuration management for Telegram and Discord plugins to ensure that agent settings are applied correctly and consistently during runtime ([elizaos-plugins/plugin-telegram](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-telegram), [elizaos-plugins/plugin-discord](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-discord)).

### Cross-Repository Coordination
- **Shared Framework Alignment**: A major project-wide effort was undertaken to align all plugins with the ESM-first architecture of the core framework. By removing legacy code formats across multiple repositories, we ensured that the entire ElizaOS ecosystem remains compatible, modular, and free of runtime errors.
- **Unified Runtime Fixes**: Coordination between the core framework and the n8n-workflow plugin allowed us to resolve deep-seated issues regarding authentication and message handling, ensuring that user settings propagate correctly from the dashboard to the agent runtime.

## Repository Spotlights

### elizaos/eliza
- Hardened the runtime for headless servers by bypassing unavailable system secret services ([#7230](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7230)).
- Introduced `@elizaos/vault` to enable secure, cross-platform secrets management ([#7197](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7197)).
- Fixed critical authentication regressions, including SIWE failures and bot token bridging ([#7288](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7288), [#7327](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7327)).
- Improved user interaction by adding clarification workflows for n8n and better context integration for Discord ([#7316](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7316), [#7315](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7315)).

### elizaos-plugins/plugin-n8n-workflow
- Formalized how agents request missing information from users by implementing `ClarificationRequest` structures ([#27](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-n8n-workflow/pull/27)).
- Improved prompt routing reliability by switching to ID-based directives ([#28](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-n8n-workflow/pull/28)).
- Resolved critical runtime crashes and message loss issues that affected Telegram bot polling ([#7244](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-n8n-workflow/issues/7244), [#7245](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-n8n-workflow/issues/7245)).

### elizaos-plugins/plugin-jupiter
- Successfully migrated to the official `api.jup.ag` endpoint to ensure service continuity ([#4](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-jupiter/pull/4)).
- Implemented mandatory API key authentication to meet modern security standards for swap requests ([#4](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-jupiter/pull/4)).

### elizaos-plugins/plugin-discord
- Addressed configuration inconsistencies where voice modules bypassed standard auto-reply settings ([#49](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-discord/issues/49), [#50](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-discord/pull/50)).

### elizaos-plugins/plugin-telegram
- Fixed a configuration issue to ensure Telegram setup tokens are correctly applied during runtime execution ([#29](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-telegram/pull/29)).

      "title": "fix(cloud): support custom image provisioning",
      "author": "Dexploarer",
      "number": 8112,
      "body": "## Summary\n- accept and return dockerImage on Eliza agent create/list/detail API responses, then pass it through sandbox creation so API-created agents can full-provision custom images\n- keep dashboard deploys wired to persisted docker images and add a dashboard e2e that creates, provisions, ticks, and observes a custom-image agent reaching running\n- harden the local cloud e2e stack and frontend bundling path used by the provisioning/audit suites\n\n## Validation\n- bun run cloud:e2e — 17 passed\n- bun run test:cloud — 473 passed, 23 skipped\n- bun run --cwd packages/cloud-infra test — 22 passed\n- bun run --cwd packages/cloud-frontend typecheck\n- bun run --cwd packages/cloud-frontend build:client\n- bun run --cwd packages/cloud-frontend audit:cloud — 116 passed\n- bun --bun playwright test tests/dashboard.spec.ts — 2 passed\n- bun --bun playwright test tests/provision.spec.ts — 2 passed\n\n<!-- greptile_comment -->\n\n<details><summary><h3>Greptile Summary</h3></summary>\n\nThis PR adds end-to-end support for custom Docker image provisioning through the Eliza cloud API: the `dockerImage` field is now accepted on agent creation, validated with a regex schema, persisted to the database, and surfaced in list and detail API responses. The dashboard is wired to pass the persisted image through sandbox creation, and new E2E tests cover both the API path and the dashboard UI flow.\n\n- **API layer** (`route.ts` for both agents and `[agentId]`): `dockerImage` is validated via a zod schema with a character-class regex, stored on create, and returned in list/detail DTOs — including the fix in `Page.tsx` that was previously hardcoding `docker_image: null`.\n- **E2E stack hardening** (`stack.ts`): migrations now run as an explicit logged step before the API server starts, `DATABASE_URL` is set earlier in the process, and the stack switches to `cloud-api-e2e-server.mjs`.\n- **Frontend fixes** (`agent-card.tsx`, `glass-composer.tsx`, `sandbox-proxy/page.tsx`, `vite.config.ts`): accessibility improvements (aria-label, aria-pressed), an inlined Vite alias for es-toolkit/compat shims, and a switch from `process.env.NODE_ENV` to `import.meta.env.PROD` for the sandbox proxy guard.\n</details>\n\n\n<h3>Confidence Score: 5/5</h3>\n\nSafe to merge — the core dockerImage feature is properly validated, typed, and covered by new E2E tests for both the API and dashboard paths.\n\nThe change is well-scoped: the new dockerImage field is validated at the API boundary with a zod regex schema, threaded through the service layer, and verified end-to-end in both provision and dashboard tests. No existing auth or billing gates are bypassed. The ancillary changes (stack hardening, frontend shims, accessibility fixes) are self-contained improvements.\n\nNo files require special attention. The telemetry guard mismatch in main.tsx is a minor inconsistency with no observable runtime impact.\n\n<details><summary><h3>Important Files Changed</h3></summary>\n\n\n\n\n| Filename | Overview |\n|----------|----------|\n| packages/cloud-api/v1/eliza/agents/route.ts | Adds dockerImage to agent create/list responses with proper zod validation (regex, trim, min/max); passed through to elizaSandboxService.createAgent correctly. |\n| packages/cloud-api/v1/eliza/agents/[agentId]/route.ts | Adds dockerImage to the agent detail response; straightforward field addition, consistent with list endpoint. |\n| packages/cloud-shared/src/lib/types/cloud-api.ts | Adds dockerImage: string | null to AgentListItemDto; AgentDetailDto inherits it via extension; AgentAdminDetailsDto already had the field. |\n| packages/cloud-frontend/src/dashboard/agents/Page.tsx | Fixes toAgentRow to pass a.dockerImage instead of hardcoded null, wiring the persisted docker image into dashboard deploys. |\n| packages/cloud-frontend/src/main.tsx | Adds telemetry opt-out via VITE_ELIZA_RENDER_TELEMETRY env var and a globalThis flag; the disable check only covers \"false\" while the underlying isRenderTelemetryEnabled() also handles \"0\" and boolean false. |\n| packages/test/cloud-e2e/src/fixtures/stack.ts | Refactors E2E stack setup: runs migrations as a logged step before starting the API server, sets DATABASE_URL earlier in the process, and switches to cloud-api-e2e-server.mjs. |\n| packages/test/cloud-e2e/tests/provision.spec.ts | Adds a full lifecycle test for API-created custom-image agents: creates, verifies persistence, provisions, polls status, and re-verifies the docker_image field. |\n| packages/test/cloud-e2e/tests/dashboard.spec.ts | Adds a dashboard E2E test that fills in the custom-image form, waits for create + provision responses, and polls the sandbox to running state. |\n| packages/ui/src/components/shell/glass-composer.tsx | Adds handleMouseDown to fire onClick immediately on pointer press and deduplicate the subsequent click event via pointerActivatedRef; the disabled guard is absent from handleMouseDown (flagged in previous review). |\n| packages/cloud-frontend/src/components/agents/agent-card.tsx | Replaces outer button wrapper with a div + absolutely-positioned inner button for click capture; improves aria-label and replaces JS hover state with CSS group-hover for remove button visibility. |\n\n</details>\n\n\n</details>\n\n\n<details><summary><h3>Sequence Diagram</h3></summary>\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Client\n    participant CloudAPI as cloud-api (POST /agents)\n    participant SandboxSvc as elizaSandboxService\n    participant DB as agent_sandboxes\n    participant ProvisionAPI as POST /agents/:id/provision\n    participant ControlPlane as Control Plane\n\n    Client->>CloudAPI: \"POST /api/v1/eliza/agents {agentName, dockerImage?}\"\n    CloudAPI->>CloudAPI: dockerImageSchema.safeParse()\n    CloudAPI->>SandboxSvc: \"createAgent({agentName, dockerImage})\"\n    SandboxSvc->>DB: \"INSERT (docker_image = dockerImage)\"\n    DB-->>SandboxSvc: agent row\n    SandboxSvc-->>CloudAPI: agent\n    CloudAPI-->>Client: \"201 {id, dockerImage}\"\n\n    Client->>ProvisionAPI: POST /api/v1/eliza/agents/:id/provision\n    ProvisionAPI->>DB: read docker_image\n    ProvisionAPI->>ControlPlane: schedule provisioning job (image)\n    ControlPlane-->>ProvisionAPI: 202 accepted\n    ProvisionAPI-->>Client: \"202 {agentId}\"\n\n    Note over Client,ControlPlane: poll GET /agents/:id until status=running\n```\n</details>\n\n\n<!-- greptile_failed_comments -->\n<details><summary><h3>Comments Outside Diff (1)</h3></summary>\n\n1. `.github/workflows/coverage-gate.yml`, line 10 ([link](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/blob/cb8cb4c0614d1f25b9cddf8a24bc2cef9237a3f0/.github/workflows/coverage-gate.yml#L10)) \n\n   <a href=\"#\"><img alt=\"P1\" src=\"https://greptile-static-assets.s3.amazonaws.com/badges/p1.svg?v=9\" align=\"top\"></a> **Lockfile integrity bypassed on failure**\n\n   The `|| bun install --no-frozen-lockfile` fallback means that whenever the lockfile is out of sync with `package.json` — the exact condition `--frozen-lockfile` is supposed to catch — CI silently falls through to an unconstrained install. Packages resolved during that fallback install may differ from what the lockfile records, making the build non-reproducible and removing the supply-chain integrity guarantee the frozen flag provides.       "id": "PR_kwDOMT5cIs7hHMrD",
      "title": "feat(orchestrator): live Claude-Code-style coding workbench (streaming, diffs, stop, SSE)",
      "author": "NubsCarson",
      "number": 8095,
      "body": "## What\nBrings the `/orchestrator` coding workbench to live, Claude-Code/Codex-style parity, end-to-end on the **opencode + Cerebras (gpt-oss-120b)** backend. Each commit is gate-green (tsgo + biome + vitest) and proven in a real headless-Chrome session.\n\n## Commits\n1. **auto-spawn opencode+cerebras on first message** — messaging a task with no live agent now spawns one (was the unimplemented `elizaos` default → `spawn ENOENT`); default framework → vendored opencode; per-task workdir.\n2. **Claude-Code-style streaming workbench view** — coalesces streamed stdout chunks into one markdown turn; renders `tool_running` events as structured tool-call cards; typed status notices; finished-session tools resolve to ✓; fixes cross-task transcript contamination.\n3. **key view-manager cards by viewType+id** — removes 9 duplicate-React-key console errors.\n4. **real interleaved diff viewer** — LCS line-diff with old/new gutters + +/-/context coloring (replaces the flat all-removals-then-all-additions); unit-tested.\n5. **Stop / Esc interrupt** — a prominent \"Agent working… / Stop (Esc)\" bar; global Esc cancels the run (proven: stopped a run in ~0.5s, work halted 5/12 files).\n6. **test fixes** — update two unit tests to the auto-spawn + opencode-default behavior.\n7. **live SSE transport** — per-task change bus + `GET /tasks/:taskId/stream` SSE endpoint + an EventSource client; the room updates live (sub-second) instead of polling, polling kept only as reconnect fallback.\n\n## Proof (headless Chrome, opencode+cerebras)\n- Tool-call cards + coalesced markdown, **0 legacy fragments**, **0 duplicate-key console errors**.\n- Real interleaved diff on a live edit (`- sugar is sweet` / `+ and so are you`).\n- Esc interrupted a run mid-work (5/12 files, ~0.5s).\n- SSE: `ready` + 23 `change` pings over a run; the workbench's EventSource subscribes and the conversation grows sub-second.\n- `tsgo` + `biome` + `vitest` (503 plugin tests) green.\n\n## Backend\nCoding backend stays **opencode + Cerebras** (vendored, gpt-oss-120b). No Claude/Codex backend.\n\n## Follow-ups (next PRs)\nInteractive permission round-trip, live plan/todo checklist, multi-backend normalization (claude/codex), and the P1 set (changed-files nav, slash menu, @-file refs, cost meter, ANSI terminal, keyboard overlay, error/retry cards).\n\n🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)\n\n<!-- greptile_comment -->\n\n<h3>Greptile Summary</h3>\n\nThis PR wires up a Claude-Code/Codex-style live coding workbench on top of the orchestrator plugin: SSE push transport replaces polling, streamed agent chunks are coalesced into coherent turns, tool calls render as structured diff/shell cards, a stop/Esc interrupt bar halts running agents, and the LCS-based `lineDiff` produces real interleaved diffs instead of flat remove-then-add blocks.\n\n- **SSE transport** (`orchestrator-routes.ts`, `client-agent.ts`): per-task pub/sub bus with `subscribeTaskChanges`; heartbeat keeps proxies alive; debounced client-side refetch on each `change` ping; polling retained as reconnect fallback.\n- **Streaming workbench** (`orchestrator-stream.tsx`): `buildConversation` merges same-lane agent chunks, collapses multi-event tool calls by ID, and resolves perpetual spinners for finished sessions; `MarkdownText` renders inline code/bold/links and fenced code blocks.\n- **Auto-spawn** (`orchestrator-task-service.ts`): messaging a task with no live agent now spawns an opencode+Cerebras session automatically; the vendored opencode backend replaces the unimplemented elizaos default.\n\n<h3>Confidence Score: 4/5</h3>\n\nSafe to merge with one fix: the EventSource client needs an onerror handler to prevent infinite reconnect loops when the task is deleted or returns a non-2xx response.\n\nThe SSE client in `streamOrchestratorTask` has no `onerror` handler. When the server returns a 404 (task not found or deleted) or any other error, the browser's EventSource automatically retries every ~3 seconds indefinitely, hammering the endpoint until the component unmounts. The polling fallback is already in place, so simply closing the EventSource on error loses no functionality. Everything else — the conversation builder, diff renderer, stop/Esc bar, cross-task contamination fix, and auto-spawn path — looks correct and well-guarded.\n\npackages/ui/src/api/client-agent.ts — the `streamOrchestratorTask` function needs an `onerror` handler that calls `source.close()`.\n\n<h3>Important Files Changed</h3>\n\n| Filename | Overview |\n|----------|----------|\n| packages/ui/src/api/client-agent.ts | Adds `streamOrchestratorTask` SSE subscription helper; missing `onerror` handler causes infinite reconnect loop on permanent server errors (404/403/5xx). |\n| plugins/plugin-agent-orchestrator/src/api/orchestrator-routes.ts | Adds GET /tasks/:taskId/stream SSE endpoint with heartbeat, per-task pub/sub, and correct cleanup on req close/error; double-cleanup is guarded correctly. |\n| plugins/plugin-agent-orchestrator/src/services/orchestrator-task-service.ts | Adds in-memory per-task change bus, auto-spawn on first message, and opencode default; `emitChange` defensively wraps subscribers; no cleanup of listeners on service stop (minor leak). |\n| plugins/plugin-agent-orchestrator/src/services/workdir-validation.ts | Adds `ensureTaskWorkdir` that creates `~/.eliza/workspaces/<taskId>` for auto-spawning; path is constructed with `path.resolve` before mkdir, staying within the workspace base. |\n| plugins/plugin-task-coordinator/src/orchestrator-stream.tsx | New file: builds the streaming conversation view — coalesces same-lane agent chunks, merges tool events by ID, renders tool-call cards, inline markdown, and structured notices. Logic is sound; FENCE regex lastIndex is reset correctly before each use. |\n| plugins/plugin-task-coordinator/src/orchestrator-diff.tsx | New file: LCS-based interleaved line diff with old/new gutter numbers; falls back to flat remove-then-add above 800 combined lines; unit-tested; keys are unique within each invocation. |\n| plugins/plugin-task-coordinator/src/OrchestratorWorkbench.tsx | Major workbench refactor: switches to `buildConversation`, adds SSE subscription, debounced refetch, adaptive poll interval, stop/Esc bar, and cross-task contamination fix; stickToBottom scroll guard is correct. |\n| packages/ui/src/components/pages/ViewManagerPage.tsx | Fixes duplicate React key warnings by compositing `viewType` + `id` in both ViewSection and TopViewsSection. |\n| plugins/plugin-agent-orchestrator/__tests__/unit/orchestrator-task-service.test.ts | Updates two tests to match auto-spawn behavior and opencode default provider; assertions are consistent with the new runtime behavior. |\n| plugins/plugin-task-coordinator/__tests__/unit/orchestrator-diff.test.ts | New unit tests for `lineDiff` covering unchanged, single-line change, insertion, deletion, and mixed-edit cases; all edge cases produce correct old/new line numbers. |\n| plugins/plugin-agent-orchestrator/src/setup-routes.ts | Registers the new SSE route path so it receives framework-level auth middleware identical to other orchestrator endpoints. |\n\n</details>\n\n<h3>Sequence Diagram</h3>\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Browser as Browser (OrchestratorWorkbench)\n    participant API as orchestrator-routes (SSE)\n    participant Service as OrchestratorTaskService\n    participant Agent as opencode Agent\n\n    Browser->>API: GET /tasks/:taskId/stream (EventSource)\n    API->>Service: subscribeTaskChanges(taskId, listener)\n    API-->>Browser: \"data: {type:\"ready\"}\"\n\n    loop Agent working\n        Agent->>Service: session event / tool_running / usage\n        Service->>Service: emitChange(taskId)\n        Service->>API: listener() fires\n        API-->>Browser: \"data: {type:\"change\"}\"\n        Browser->>Browser: scheduleRefetch (debounce 150ms)\n        Browser->>API: GET /tasks/:taskId/messages + events\n        API-->>Browser: updated conversation tail\n        Browser->>Browser: buildConversation → render\n    end\n\n    Browser->>API: Stop (Esc / button)\n    API->>Service: stopOrchestratorAgent(taskId, sessionId)\n    Service->>Agent: terminate\n\n    Browser->>Browser: component unmount / task switch\n    Browser->>API: EventSource.close()\n    API->>API: req close → cleanup (clearInterval + unsubscribe + res.end)\n```\n\n<sub>Reviews (6): Last reviewed commit: [\"feat(orchestrator): premium UI pass — la...\"](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/commit/63f569ecf8e9e8b7ba0799c6d27fae203a1405e0) | [Re-trigger Greptile](https://app.greptile.com/api/retrigger?id=34800292)</sub>\n\n<!-- /greptile_comment -->",
